hypothesis: Incorporating physics-based regularization constraints (non-negativity and sum-to-one) directly into the diffusion model loss function will produce composite material designs with higher validity rates (both physical and fabrication) and comparable or better fidelity and diversity compared to unconstrained generative models (CVAE and TabDDPM), because embedding domain-specific physical laws as inductive biases guides the generative process toward physically realizable solutions without sacrificing sample quality.
